rulings.Case Title: GARRITT BLANZ ET AL VS. ROBERT C HINCKLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulation For Settlement Under Ccp Section 664.6 [Per Email Dated 12/7/2021]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT UNDER CCP SECTION 664.6 Status Update. If the issues have been resolved or parties seek another continuance, parties shall notify the Court via e-mail by 4:00 p.m. on February 24, 2022, otherwise hearing required and all counsel to appear in-person. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA WILSON MARKHAM VS. ANGELO WILSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to causes of action asserted by plaintiff, as individual, is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege facts, or suggest any possibility of amendment, to establish standing at this time. Beckwith v. Dahl (2012) 205 Cal. App. 4th 1039 factually distinguishable. Demurrer to the causes of action brought by Plaintiff, as trustee, is overruled. Defendant fails to cite any authority for its argument and neither the Trust nor the probate files establish that Carrie Wilson's conservator is the trustee of the operative trust. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FREDY MARTINEZ VS. REAL TIME RESOLUTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-21-668264 WITH CASE CGC-21-596717
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2022 line 6. PLAINTIFF FREDY MARTINEZ'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-21-668264 WITH CASE CGC-21-596717 Hearing Required. No later than February 25, 2022 by 9:00 a.m. Defendant Real Time Resolution to provide (file and serve with a hard courtesy copy delivered to the department) the following evidence: (1) 6 monthly mortgage bills for the months immediately preceding the recordation of the Notice of Default or a ledger reflecting the amounts owed/billed and amounts received for those months; (2) insurance bills and proof of payment for the periods already paid, if any; and (3) tax bills and proof of payment for the periods already paid, if any. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LPF YERBA BUENA, INC. VS. KEVIN GRAHN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2022 line 9. DEFENDANT KEVIN GRAHN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. Notice states that rent for October 2021 was $2,600, but demands a total of $2,660, i.e. $60 more than the amount of rent due for October 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
